title: iris kirkwoodiae subsp. calcarea
intro: Iris kirkwoodiae subsp. calcarea, also known as the limestone iris, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant found in the mountainous regions of the western Cape Province in South Africa. The limestone iris has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be attributed to its high content of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of the limestone iris include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, the limestone iris is typically used in the form of a dried and powdered root, which can be consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies.
